title: checkout-service p99 latency + 5xx spike after v2.31.0 deploy
alertText:
[PagerDuty] TRIGGERED · SEV2 · checkout-service
High p99 latency (datadog-monitor #48213)
p99 = 2.41s over last 5m (warn 800ms / crit 2s) · sustained > 5m
HTTP 5xx error rate = 6.8% (baseline 0.1%) · 1,247 failed POST /api/v1/checkout in 5m
Env: production · Region: us-east-1 · Cluster: prod-use1-a
Correlated change: deploy checkout-service v2.31.0 @ 14:02 UTC (1 release in window)
Ack: @oncall-checkout · Opened 14:07 UTC
dashboardSpec: Grafana 'checkout-service / overview': p99 latency flat ~120ms then a vertical climb to ~2.4s at the 14:02 deploy marker; 5xx rate fans to 6.8%; HikariPool active pins at 100/100 with pending climbing; requests/sec flat ~1.2k (no traffic surge).
logs:
14:02:08  checkout-service  INFO   Rolling update started: v2.30.4 -> v2.31.0 (kubectl, 4 replicas)
14:02:41  checkout-service  INFO   Started CheckoutApplication in 6.41s, listening on :8080
14:02:41  checkout-service  INFO   HikariPool-1 - configuration: maximumPoolSize=100, minimumIdle=100, connectionTimeout=30000  (prev release v2.30.4: maximumPoolSize=20)
14:03:01  postgres-primary  LOG    FATAL: sorry, too many clients already
14:03:01  checkout-service  WARN   HikariPool-1 - Pool stats (total=100, active=100, idle=0, waiting=37)
14:03:02  checkout-service  ERROR  POST /api/v1/checkout 500 : org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: FATAL: sorry, too many clients already
14:03:04  checkout-service  ERROR  POST /api/v1/checkout 500 : java.sql.SQLTransientConnectionException: HikariPool-1 - Connection is not available, request timed out after 30001ms
14:03:09  postgres-primary  LOG    FATAL: remaining connection slots are reserved for non-replication superuser connections
14:03:18  postgres-primary  LOG    parameter "max_connections" = 100 (shared across all clients; 4 checkout replicas x 100 pool = 400 requested)
14:03:55  checkout-service  WARN   HikariPool-1 - Pool stats (total=100, active=100, idle=0, waiting=58)
14:05:12  postgres-primary  LOG    FATAL: sorry, too many clients already
14:06:55  redis-cache       INFO   evicted_keys=0, used_memory=412MB/4GB, hit_rate=0.998  (nominal)
runbookMarkdown:
# checkout-service — On-call Runbook

## 1. High latency / 5xx immediately after a deploy
Applies when symptoms begin AT a deploy annotation. Treat the release as prime suspect. Fastest safe mitigation: `kubectl rollout undo deploy/checkout-service`.

## 2. Database connection pool exhaustion
Symptoms: `HikariPool-1` timeouts, pool `active == max` with `idle=0` and growing `waiting`, Postgres `FATAL: sorry, too many clients already` / `remaining connection slots are reserved`.
Postgres `max_connections = 100`, shared across ALL services and replicas. With N replicas, demand = N x pool `maximumPoolSize`. Most common trigger: a release that RAISED `maximumPoolSize`. Mitigate by rolling back the change or lowering the pool size. Do NOT raise `max_connections` under load.

## 3. Redis cache evictions (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
Applies ONLY when `redis_evicted_keys` is climbing. If Redis is green (`evicted_keys=0`), not applicable.

## 4. CDN / edge 4xx (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
Applies to edge 4xx, not origin 5xx on POST routes.
groundTruthRootCause: Deploy v2.31.0 raised the HikariCP DB connection pool maximumPoolSize from 20 to 100. Across 4 replicas the fleet requested up to 400 connections against a Postgres primary whose max_connections is 100 (shared), exhausting connection slots and producing connection-timeout latency and 5xx. Config-induced pool exhaustion, not a leak — the smoking gun is the pool-size delta in the v2.31.0 config log.
expectedSlackUpdate: :rotating_light: SEV2 — checkout-service degraded (us-east-1). p99 2.4s, 5xx 6.8% since 14:02 UTC. Root cause: deploy v2.31.0 raised DB pool 20->100; 4 replicas exhausted Postgres max_connections=100. Action: rolling back to v2.30.4 (reversible). ETA ~5 min. Next update 14:20. IC: @oncall-checkout
